title: Dog Mastitis Guide: Hard, Hot Mammary Glands and Triage Advice
meta_desc: Learn about dog mastitis symptoms, why hard mammary glands are a high-urgency emergency, and how photos help your vet triage your pet quickly.
content_html:
What is Dog Mastitis?

Mastitis is an inflammation or bacterial infection of the mammary glands. This condition is most common in nursing dogs but can occasionally occur during a false pregnancy or in any female dog. The infection causes the milk ducts to become blocked and inflamed, leading to glands that feel firm, swollen, and abnormally hot to the touch. In severe cases, the milk may appear discolored, bloody, or thick.

Is it an Emergency?

The urgency level for mastitis is High. This condition is extremely painful and can escalate rapidly into a life-threatening systemic infection known as sepsis. If your dog is lethargic, refusing to eat, has a fever, or if the mammary glands are turning dark purple or black (indicating tissue death), you must seek emergency veterinary care immediately. Prompt treatment with antibiotics and pain management is essential to prevent the infection from spreading to the bloodstream.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

When communicating with a veterinary professional, a high-quality photo of the affected area is incredibly valuable. Visual evidence allows a triage nurse or veterinarian to assess the degree of inflammation, look for signs of abscesses, and identify skin discoloration that may indicate gangrenous mastitis. Seeing the area helps the clinical team determine how quickly your dog needs to be seen and prepares them for the specific type of care required upon your arrival.

faqs.question: Can my puppies still nurse if the mother has mastitis?
faqs.answer: In many cases, nursing is actually encouraged to help drain the gland, but only if the mother is not in too much pain and the milk is not toxic. You must consult your veterinarian first, as some antibiotics are unsafe for puppies and severely infected milk can make them sick.

faqs.question: What are the early signs of mastitis?
faqs.answer: Early signs include mild swelling of a single mammary gland, the gland feeling warmer than the rest of the body, and the mother acting protective or sensitive when the puppies attempt to nurse.

faqs.question: How is dog mastitis usually treated?
faqs.answer: Treatment typically involves a course of pet-safe antibiotics, anti-inflammatory pain medication, and sometimes 'milk stripping' or warm compresses to encourage drainage and relieve pressure.
